output: Verification completed, but T10 is blocked rather than green. Focused suites passed (`71/71` tests across 12 files), `npm test` passed (`4558/4558` tests across `881/881` files), and `npx tsc --noEmit` is clean. A real Chromium browser harness was run and its notes were saved to `.megaplan/plans/milestone-m1a-from-docs-state-20260416-0928/browser_check.json`; that check confirmed the model select path and measured `EditModePanel` initial open at 1 render, but the textarea interaction did not pass in the browser harness because only the first typed character persisted. The render-reduction gate is also still evidence-blocked because `docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md` explicitly says the required pre-change bug-path baseline was never captured, so the mandated 75% comparison cannot be verified from repo evidence. The temporary repro script/harness was deleted after execution.

task_updates.executor_notes: Focused verification and the full repository suite are green, and `npx tsc --noEmit` is clean. Focused suites passed (`71/71` across 12 files), and the full suite passed with `881/881` files and `4558/4558` tests. A real Chromium browser harness was run with a temporary repro script, and that script was deleted afterward; the resulting notes were saved to `.megaplan/plans/milestone-m1a-from-docs-state-20260416-0928/browser_check.json`. That browser check verified the model select path and measured `EditModePanel` initial open at 1 render, but it did not clear the textarea gate: typing into the edit textarea only persisted the first character (`"t"`) in the harness. The hard gate also remains evidence-blocked on the render-reduction comparison because `docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md` still explicitly says the bug-path pre-change baseline was never captured, so the required 75% reduction claim cannot be verified from repo evidence. Because the browser/manual gate and baseline-comparison gate are not green end-to-end, T10 is blocked rather than complete.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: No. The automated suites and typecheck are green, but the end-to-end gate is still blocked. The real-browser repro harness notes saved in `browser_check.json` show the model select path succeeded while the textarea path did not: only the first character persisted in the harness. Separately, the required bug-path pre-change render baseline is still missing from repo evidence, so the mandated 75% reduction comparison cannot be verified. The temporary browser repro script was run and then deleted.
